title: nepenthes cornuta
intro: Nepenthes cornuta is a species of carnivorous plant, also known as a pitcher plant, that belongs to the Nepenthaceae family. This plant is native to the Philippines and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Nepenthes cornuta include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
